energy_status: True energy metrics are unavailable on iOS Simulator; Apple Power Profiler is unsupported there. CPU cycles are retained as the stable on-simulator energy proxy while direct device GPU and energy reports live under benchmarks/uikit-device/.

layers.label: Engine Microbenchmarks
notes: UIKit engine coverage currently spans primitive views, animation effects, and primitive lifecycle slices.

layers.label: Representative Screen Flows
notes: Flow coverage now spans launch/lifecycle and user-journey cases, but hitch and refresh-mode matrices are still incomplete.

layers.label: OS-Bridge Benchmarks
notes: Bridge coverage measures app-owned wrapper overhead separately from system-owned UI surfaces.

styles.label: Idiomatic UIKit
notes: Idiomatic retained-view parity is the default UIKit baseline in this suite.

styles.label: Hand-Optimized UIKit
notes: The optimized UIKit slice now covers the full currently implemented journey, bridge, and endurance families, plus primitive-lifecycle, animation-effect, image-pipeline, and large-editor text-input peers; launch/lifecycle, layout/invalidation, authoring, component microbenchmarks, and stress/pathological traps still need tuned peers.

battery.label: Launch & Lifecycle
notes: The XCTest harness now runs simple-home and heavy-home cold launch, detail-route launch, warm resume, and foreground-after-background batteries, using XCTApplicationLaunchMetric on the cold launch cases.

battery.label: Primitive Mount / Update / Destroy
notes: Flat rects, labels, cards, images, an empty-root slice, a shared control-set slice, and retained-view remove-all/remount slices are all covered.

battery.label: Layout & Invalidation
notes: Flat-grid rotation, deep-stack theme swap, and safe-area inset relayout batteries are all implemented.

battery.label: Text & Text Input
notes: Large-editor keystroke, paste, and selection-replace workloads now complement the existing UILabel and form-journey coverage.

battery.label: Image Pipeline
notes: The committed UIKit image battery now splits PNG decode, upload/attach, and first-visible phases into separate persisted workloads.

battery.label: Lists, Grids, & Chat
notes: Feed, thumbnail-grid, and chat-thread scroll matrices now exist alongside collection encode and navigation slices.

battery.label: Navigation & Input Latency
notes: Direct button-press, slider-scrub, and text-focus response batteries now complement the higher-level journey cases.

battery.label: Animation & Visual Effects
notes: Idiomatic and hand-tuned animation-effect cases now exist, but the suite still lacks full hitch-ratio and refresh-mode matrices on real 60 Hz and ProMotion hardware.

battery.label: State Mutation & Reconciliation
notes: Single-node, 1 percent, 10 percent, and full-theme tree mutation batteries now expose diff/apply cost directly.

battery.label: OS Bridge Overhead
notes: Permission, sensor, photo import, file import, share payload, and localhost transport/render bridge workloads are all covered without claiming system-owned UI as a renderer win.

battery.label: Endurance, Memory, & Thermal Drift
notes: Open/close, tab-switch, and idle-animation endurance loops are now part of the committed UIKit battery.

battery.label: Stress & Pathological Regressions
notes: Dedicated 10k-node, 300-animation, and 100 Hz ticker traps now complement the rest of the UIKit suite.
The UIKit reports now persist explicit contract coverage so the suite does not over-claim comprehensiveness.

The simulator report remains proxy-only for CPU, memory, and storage. Phase signposts stay instrumented in the shared harness, but Xcode 26 simulator app-hosted XCTest runs are currently not collecting them through XCTOSSignpostMetric because that path is crashing in Apple metric teardown. Device GPU and energy numbers live in the device report.
